draft: ISO/IEC DIS 24970:2025 — Artificial intelligence — AI system logging
published: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — AI management system (governance controls map to Art. 12)
upcoming: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 12 (expected Q4 2026)

obligations.source_quote: High-risk AI systems shall technically allow for the automatic recording of events (logs) over the lifetime of the system.
obligations.addressee_note: Provider of a high-risk AI system (as defined in Art. 3(3))

decomposed_atoms.description: The AI system must have a technical mechanism for recording events
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The system SHALL have logging functionality built in — not optional, not a plugin, but a core technical capability

decomposed_atoms.description: Logging must happen automatically without manual intervention
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Events SHALL be recorded automatically — not requiring a human to manually trigger logging

decomposed_atoms.description: What is logged must be 'events' (discrete occurrences)
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The system SHALL record discrete events, not just aggregate metrics or summaries

decomposed_atoms.description: Logging must operate from deployment to decommission
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logging SHALL be active for the entire operational lifetime of the system — from first deployment to final shutdown

automation_assessment.detection_method: Static analysis: check for logging framework imports, logging calls in critical paths
automation_assessment.rationale: Whether a system has automatic logging is a binary technical fact, fully determinable from code analysis

recital_alignment.relevant_excerpt: high-risk AI systems should technically allow for the automatic recording of events, by means of logs, over the duration of the lifetime of the system
recital_alignment.additional_context: Recital 71 adds that this is to 'enable traceability' and 'verify compliance'

obligations.source_quote: logging capabilities shall enable the recording of events relevant for: (a) identifying situations that may result in the high-risk AI system presenting a risk within the meaning of Article 79(1) or in a substantial modification;

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs must capture events that could indicate the system is presenting a risk
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL include events that help identify when the system may be operating outside its intended parameters or causing harm

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs must capture events related to substantial modifications
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL record events that indicate the system has undergone or is undergoing a substantial change in behavior or capability

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check if logging covers: error states, anomaly detection, model drift, performance degradation, input/output distribution shifts
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: What constitutes a 'risk' or 'substantial modification' is context-dependent — depends on the specific AI system's intended purpose
automation_assessment.rationale: We can check IF risk-related events are logged, but cannot fully determine if ALL relevant risk events are covered without understanding the specific system's risk profile

cross_references.context: Defines when an AI system 'presents a risk' — when it affects health, safety, fundamental rights, environment, democracy, rule of law, or leads to non-compliance

obligations.source_quote: logging capabilities shall enable the recording of events relevant for: (b) facilitating the post-market monitoring referred to in Article 72;

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs must contain data useful for ongoing monitoring after deployment
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL include sufficient operational data to support post-market monitoring activities (performance metrics, usage patterns, incident data)

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for: performance metric logging, usage statistics collection, error rate tracking, feedback loop mechanisms
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: What constitutes 'sufficient' monitoring data depends on the system's risk profile and Art. 72 monitoring plan
automation_assessment.rationale: Can verify presence of monitoring infrastructure but cannot determine completeness without the specific monitoring plan

cross_references.context: Post-market monitoring system requirements — provider must establish proportionate monitoring

obligations.source_quote: logging capabilities shall enable the recording of events relevant for: (c) monitoring the operation of high-risk AI systems referred to in Article 26(5).

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs must provide deployers with operational monitoring capability
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The logging system SHALL produce data that deployers can use to monitor the AI system's operation (as required by Art. 26(5))

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs or log summaries must be accessible to the deployer
decomposed_atoms.requirement: The provider SHALL make relevant log data available to deployers — either directly or through a monitoring interface

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for: log export capability, monitoring API/dashboard, deployer-facing log access controls
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the log format and access mechanism is adequate for deployer monitoring
automation_assessment.rationale: Deployer operational monitoring cannot be verified from provider code — requires human review of deployer access controls

cross_references.context: Deployer obligation to monitor operation based on instructions of use — requires provider to enable this

obligations.source_quote: For high-risk AI systems referred to in point 1 (a), of Annex III, the logging capabilities shall provide, at a minimum: (a) recording of the period of each use of the system (start date and time and end date and time of each use);
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))

decomposed_atoms.description: Each use session must have start and end timestamps
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL record the start date/time and end date/time of every use session

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for session tracking with timestamp logging (start/end events)
automation_assessment.rationale: Session timestamp logging is a binary technical check — either session start/end is logged or it isn't

obligations.source_quote: the reference database against which input data has been checked by the system;
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs must identify which database was used for matching
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL record a reference/identifier for the database used in each matching operation

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check that database identifier is included in matching operation logs
automation_assessment.rationale: Whether database reference is logged is deterministic

obligations.source_quote: the input data for which the search has led to a match;
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))

decomposed_atoms.description: When a match occurs, the input that caused the match must be logged
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL record the input data that resulted in a positive match (note: GDPR constraints on storing biometric data apply)

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check that match events include input reference/hash
automation_assessment.rationale: Whether matched input is logged is deterministic. Note: implementation may use hashes rather than raw biometric data for GDPR compliance.

obligations.source_quote: the identification of the natural persons involved in the verification of the results, as referred to in Article 14(5).
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))

decomposed_atoms.description: The person who verified AI results must be identified in logs
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Logs SHALL record the identity of the natural person(s) who verified the system's results

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for user/operator ID logging on verification/approval actions
automation_assessment.rationale: Whether human verifier identity is logged is deterministic

cross_references.context: Human oversight — results of biometric identification must be verified by at least two natural persons

obligations.source_quote: the logs shall be kept for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at least six months, unless provided otherwise in applicable Union or national law, in particular in Union law on the protection of personal data.
obligations.note: This obligation is in Art. 19 but directly extends Art. 12 logging requirements

decomposed_atoms.description: Logs must be kept for at least 6 months
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Provider SHALL retain automatically generated logs for a minimum of 6 months

decomposed_atoms.description: Retention period should match the system's intended purpose
decomposed_atoms.requirement: If the system's purpose warrants longer retention, logs SHALL be kept for a period appropriate to that purpose (6 months is the floor, not the ceiling)

decomposed_atoms.description: Retention must comply with GDPR and other data protection law
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Log retention SHALL comply with applicable data protection law — if GDPR requires deletion before 6 months for certain personal data, that takes precedence

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for: log retention configuration, log rotation policies, TTL settings, archive policies
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether 6 months is sufficient for the specific system's purpose, and whether retention conflicts with GDPR data minimization
automation_assessment.rationale: Can verify if retention policy exists and meets 6-month minimum, but appropriateness to purpose requires human judgment

obligations.source_quote: Providers that are financial institutions subject to requirements regarding their internal governance, arrangements or processes under Union financial services law shall maintain the logs automatically generated by their high-risk AI systems as part of the documentation kept under the relevant financial services law.
obligations.scope_limitation: Only applies to providers that are financial institutions subject to Union financial services law

decomposed_atoms.description: Financial institution providers must keep AI logs as part of their financial services documentation
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Provider financial institutions SHALL maintain AI system logs as part of the documentation required under Union financial services law

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check if system is operated by a financial institution — requires human confirmation
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether the provider is a financial institution and which financial services law applies
automation_assessment.rationale: Cannot determine from code whether the operator is a financial institution

obligations.source_quote: Deployers of high-risk AI systems shall keep the logs automatically generated by that high-risk AI system to the extent such logs are under their control, for a period appropriate to the intended purpose of the high-risk AI system, of at least six months, unless provided otherwise in applicable Union or national law, in particular in Union law on the protection of personal data.
obligations.scope_limitation: Deployer obligation — applies only when scanning as deployer, not provider

decomposed_atoms.description: Deployer must retain AI-generated logs for at least 6 months
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Deployer SHALL keep automatically generated logs for at least 6 months, appropriate to the intended purpose

decomposed_atoms.description: Deployer retention must comply with data protection law
decomposed_atoms.requirement: Deployer log retention SHALL comply with applicable data protection law (GDPR)

automation_assessment.detection_method: Check for log retention config — same as ART19-OBL-1 but from deployer perspective
automation_assessment.human_judgment_needed: Whether deployer has control over logs and whether retention period is appropriate
automation_assessment.rationale: Technical retention check is same as provider; but deployer control scope requires human judgment

scope_notes.general_obligations: ART12-OBL-1, ART12-OBL-2a, ART12-OBL-2b, ART12-OBL-2c, ART19-OBL-1 — apply to ALL high-risk AI systems
scope_notes.biometric_only: ART12-OBL-3a, ART12-OBL-3b, ART12-OBL-3c, ART12-OBL-3d — apply ONLY to biometric identification systems (Annex III, point 1(a))
